title: Fix Firebase Auth and Firestore persistence on React Native
description: Configure platform-conditional Firebase initialization: use initializeAuth with AsyncStorage persistence on native, keep getAuth on web. Remove persistentLocalCache on native (IndexedDB unavailable).

steps.name: Regression test for platform-specific Firebase initialization
steps.description: Write tests that verify Firebase Auth uses initializeAuth with getReactNativePersistence(AsyncStorage) on native platforms, and that Firestore does not use persistentLocalCache on native. Tests should fail against current code.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies getFirebaseAuth() calls initializeAuth with ReactNativePersistence on native
Test verifies getFirebaseDb() does not pass persistentLocalCache on native
Test verifies web platform behavior is unchanged (getAuth, persistentLocalCache)
quality_gates: All new tests fail against current code (RED)
steps.implementation_notes: Mock Platform.OS to test both native and web paths. Use jest.isolateModules to get fresh config per test.

steps.name: Platform-conditional Firebase initialization
steps.description: Modify firebase-config.ts to use initializeAuth with getReactNativePersistence(AsyncStorage) on native platforms (Platform.OS !== 'web'). On native, omit persistentLocalCache from Firestore config. Preserve existing web behavior.
acceptance_criteria: On native: Auth initialized with initializeAuth + getReactNativePersistence(AsyncStorage)
On native: Firestore initialized without persistentLocalCache
On web: Auth initialized with getAuth (unchanged)
On web: Firestore initialized with persistentLocalCache (unchanged)
quality_gates: All tests pass including regression tests
steps.implementation_notes: Use Platform.OS from react-native for detection. Import AsyncStorage and getReactNativePersistence conditionally.

validation.reason: Minimal bugfix scope - 2 steps following RED-GREEN pattern
